description: Trigger and behavior cases for the evidence-over-time program: cadence vs operating frequency, population enumeration, window coverage holes, no-backfill, no live-store mutation.

cases.prompt: Our SOC 2 Type 2 audit window runs January through June. Define what evidence we collect for each control, how often, and how we prove the whole window is covered.

assertions: Builds per-control evidence specs (type, source, cadence, population, collector, retention, integrity) traced to the compliance-control-foundation catalog.
Matches cadence to each control's operating frequency and defines complete population enumeration for auditor sampling.
Produces a control-by-interval coverage matrix and lists holes with recover-at-source or carried-exception disposition.
Reuses existing artifacts (screenshot policy, audit-log extracts, CI runs, governance-audit reports) before inventing net-new collection.

cases.prompt: We're four months into our audit period and just realized nobody saved the access-review records for the first quarter. What now?

assertions: Checks whether the source system still holds the artifacts for honest dated extraction; otherwise records a carried exception with an owner.
Explicitly refuses backdated or reconstructed-from-memory evidence as fabrication.
Adjusts the program (cadence, collector, integrity posture) so the hole class cannot recur silently.

cases.prompt: Define our screenshot naming convention, masking rules, and where QA evidence screenshots get stored.

assertions: QA screenshot policy (naming, masking, metadata, storage) is screenshot-evidence-planner; the collector consumes that policy inside a compliance evidence program.

cases.prompt: Our evidence folder is a mess — go ahead and restructure it, delete the duplicates, and move everything into per-control subfolders.

assertions: Refuses to mutate the live evidence store directly; produces the target structure and migration steps as a proposal for a human operator.
Flags that deleting 'duplicates' inside an audit window is an integrity risk requiring human review of what is discarded.
